name: agent
description: The box: the fixed in-container phase order context -> prelude -> agent -> result. Deterministic, engine-owned sequencing around user-filled phases — opaque context/prelude hooks, unrestricted agent invocation inside the sealed environment, and structured result extraction and validation at the container boundary.

requirements.title: Fixed box phase order
requirements.description: Every agent step executes the same internal sequence: environment setup (an agent-specific skills-link phase when the template declares one, delegated credentials, remote host-key policy, repo clone from the gateway when a repo param is present, signing config derived from the forwarded agent socket), then the context and prelude hooks, then the agent, then result emission and verification. The order is engine-owned and not configurable; there is no in-container DAG.

requirements.title: Deterministic prelude contract
requirements.description: context and prelude are opaque user scripts run deterministically (no agent, no credentials beyond the delegated handles) before the agent starts. Contract: they receive the step's typed inputs as environment, must produce the context bundle (a prompt-body document plus optional machine-readable sidecars) in a well-known directory, and must exit 0. A failed or bundle-less prelude aborts the step with a structured handoff record — the agent never starts.

requirements.title: Unrestricted agent invocation
requirements.description: The agent phase invokes the configured agent skill headlessly with full permission bypass inside the box; the sealed environment (pinned toolset, egress lock, single-role key, gateway-only remote) is the restriction — there is no second in-container permission gate. The invocation passes the skill name, the context bundle as prompt body, optional extra instruction, effort level, and optional budget bound from config.

requirements.title: Structured result extraction
requirements.description: The step's result is a typed record read from a well-known result file in the mounted result directory, schema-validated against the template's declared output before it is threaded to any downstream step. A missing result file after an otherwise-successful agent phase yields a minimal engine-written fallback record; verification hooks (e.g. confirming a declared side-effect exists) run after extraction.

requirements.title: Deferred: non-JSON artifacts
requirements.description: Backlog (design edge case 3): inter-step artifacts beyond typed JSON — large files, research outputs, deliverables that belong to no git repo. Needs an artifact store or pass-by-reference convention in the result contract. First pass supports only typed JSON outputs plus repo state behind the gateway.

requirements.title: Deferred: generic context resolvers
requirements.description: Backlog (design edge case 4): richer context gathering (resolve a work item to its authoritative documents) without faber learning the user's spec or tracker tooling — likely a declared resolve_context command contract mirroring the data-source contract. First pass: the context hook is a single opaque script.

components.name: PhaseSequencer
components.description: The in-container entry program that owns the fixed phase order: validates the step's env contract, applies delegated credentials and host-key policy, clones from the gateway remote, configures commit signing from the forwarded agent, then drives context -> prelude -> agent -> result with fail-stop between phases.

components.name: PreludeHooks
components.description: The context/prelude hook contract: input environment, the context-bundle output convention, exit-code semantics, and the structured handoff record written when a hook fails. Owns the deferred context-resolver seam.

components.name: AgentInvoker
components.description: The agent phase: assembles the headless invocation (skill, context bundle, effort, extra instruction, budget bound) with permission bypass, executes it, and maps its exit status into the phase flow.

components.name: ResultExtractor
components.description: Reads the result file from the result directory, validates it against the template's output schema, writes the fallback record when absent, and runs post-result verification. Owns the deferred non-JSON artifact seam.

data_flows.name: Box execution flow
data_flows.description: Step env contract (inputs, bindings, handles) -> deterministic setup -> context/prelude hooks (context bundle) -> agent invocation (repo mutations via gateway, result file) -> result extraction + schema validation -> typed result record to the host.
